{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Auditor",
  "description": "Eta Zuma Mining and Industries Limited provides premium solutions to businesses in the primary and secondary sectors of the economy, especially those in solid minerals, extractive industry, manufacturing industry and agriculture. Some of its clients are Zuma 828 Coal Limited, IMEX Mineral Resources and Processing Company, Zuma Energy Nigeria Limited, Zuma Steel W/A Limited, and Zuma Metals &amp; Energy Resources Limited

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor
Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja&nbsp; (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Internal Auditor is responsible for leading and executing internal audit engagements across the organization, ensuring compliance with internal policies, regulatory standards, and industry best practices.
	The role involves assessing financial and operational controls, identifying risks, and providing actionable recommendations to strengthen governance, improve efficiency, and mitigate business risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the development and execution of the annual internal audit plan
	Lead and perform risk-based financial, operational, and compliance audits
	Plan audit assignments, including defining scope, objectives, and audit programs
	Conduct audit fieldwork, review findings, and ensure proper documentation
	Lead opening and closing meetings with auditees and key stakeholders
	Identify control deficiencies, inefficiencies, and risk exposures, and recommend corrective actions
	Evaluate financial records, inventory systems, procurement, and operational processes
	Perform data analysis and control testing to validate audit findings
	Prepare and present detailed audit reports to Management
	Monitor and follow up on implementation of audit recommendations
	Participate in process improvement initiatives and advise on internal controls
	Conduct ad hoc audits and investigations into suspected irregularities
	Mentor and guide junior auditors where applicable
	Ensure audit activities comply with internal audit standards and company policies


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field
	5&ndash;8 years of relevant experience in internal/external audit
	Professional certification: ICAN, ACCA, CIA, or CPA (required or nearing completion)
	Strong experience in operational audits (inventory, logistics, procurement)
	Working knowledge of ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle) and audit tools
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent report writing and stakeholder communication skills
	High level of integrity, independence, and professionalism.

Internal Auditor Job at Eta Zuma Mining and Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Eta Zuma Mining and Industries Limited provides premium solutions to businesses in the primary and secondary sectors of the economy, especially those in solid minerals, extractive industry, manufacturing industry and agriculture. Some of its clients are Zuma 828 Coal Limited, IMEX Mineral Resources and Processing Company, Zuma Energy Nigeria Limited, Zuma Steel W/A Limited, and Zuma Metals & Energy Resources Limited

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Auditor
Job Location: Abuja  (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Internal Auditor is responsible for leading and executing internal audit engagements across the organization, ensuring compliance with internal policies, regulatory standards, and industry best practices.
	The role involves assessing financial and operational controls, identifying risks, and providing actionable recommendations to strengthen governance, improve efficiency, and mitigate business risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the development and execution of the annual internal audit plan
	Lead and perform risk-based financial, operational, and compliance audits
	Plan audit assignments, including defining scope, objectives, and audit programs
	Conduct audit fieldwork, review findings, and ensure proper documentation
	Lead opening and closing meetings with auditees and key stakeholders
	Identify control deficiencies, inefficiencies, and risk exposures, and recommend corrective actions
	Evaluate financial records, inventory systems, procurement, and operational processes
	Perform data analysis and control testing to validate audit findings
	Prepare and present detailed audit reports to Management
	Monitor and follow up on implementation of audit recommendations
	Participate in process improvement initiatives and advise on internal controls
	Conduct ad hoc audits and investigations into suspected irregularities
	Mentor and guide junior auditors where applicable
	Ensure audit activities comply with internal audit standards and company policies


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related field
	5–8 years of relevant experience in internal/external audit
	Professional certification: ICAN, ACCA, CIA, or CPA (required or nearing completion)
	Strong experience in operational audits (inventory, logistics, procurement)
	Working knowledge of ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle) and audit tools
	Strong analytical, investigative, and problem-solving skills
	Excellent report writing and stakeholder communication skills
	High level of integrity, independence, and professionalism.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Digital Content & Community Manager",
  "description": "Pulse is Africa&rsquo;s leading innovative media company, informing and engaging Africa&rsquo;s young audience - and providing expansive media reach and creative marketing solutions to partners. We run news websites and social channels and marketing and production across Francophone and Anglophone West Africa and East Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Content &amp; Community Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
Content Creation &amp; Scheduling:


	Develop and execute a consistent content calendar across all relevant social media platforms, aligned with event timelines and marketing objectives.
	Write compelling, on-brand copy for posts, captions, stories, reels, etc. on all digital touchpoints
	Brief the designer on visual content requirements, providing clear direction on messaging, tone, and creative intent
	Review and approve all content for brand consistency, accuracy, and quality before publishing
	Repurpose event content &mdash; highlights, testimonials, behind-the-scenes moments &mdash; into evergreen digital assets


Community Management:


	Manage all community channels including social media inboxes, comment sections, WhatsApp broadcast lists, and any owned community platforms
	Respond to direct messages, comments, and enquiries in a timely, warm, and on-brand manner
	Monitor audience sentiment and flag recurring feedback, concerns, or trends to the wider team
	Foster genuine connection and loyalty within the community through consistent, meaningful engagement
	Grow the audience organically through strategic engagement, collaborations, and community-driven campaigns


Live Event Social Media Coverage:


	Lead real-time social media coverage during events, capturing and publishing content that brings the live experience to audiences online
	Coordinate with photographers, videographers, and the wider content team on event day to ensure a steady flow of high-quality assets
	Publish live updates, stories, and reels during events to maximise reach and engagement in real time
	Manage any event-specific hashtags, geotags, and digital activations to drive audience participation


Audience Growth &amp; Engagement Strategy:


	Develop and implement pre- and post-event digital campaigns to build anticipation, drive ticket sales, and sustain audience interest between events
	Identify and engage relevant influencers, brand advocates, and community voices to amplify event reach
	Track and analyse content performance metrics and translate insights into actionable improvements
	Contribute to post-event reports with digital performance data including reach, engagement, follower growth, and sentiment


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Communications, Media Studies, or a related field
	3 &ndash; 5 years of experience in social media management, digital content creation, or community management
	Proven experience managing social media for events, lifestyle brands, or entertainment-focused organizations.
	Strong portfolio demonstrating content creation across multiple platforms
	Experience with live event social media coverage is a strong advantage


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Excellent copywriting skills with a strong sense of tone, voice, and audience
	Deep understanding of social media platforms, algorithms, and content best practices
	Strong visual sensibility and ability to brief and collaborate effectively with designers
	Ability to read analytics and draw meaningful insights
	Highly organised with the ability to manage a content calendar across multiple events simultaneously
	Energetic, personable, and genuinely passionate about community building
	Proficient in social media management tools, Canva or Adobe Suite (basic), and analytics platforms.


Benefits


	Competitive salary based on experience
	Hybrid work mode
	Health Insurance Cover
	Group Life Insurance cover
	Contributory Pension Scheme
	?Data and airtime monthly allowance.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Digital Content & Community Manager Job at Pulse Nigeria




        
    

        
        Pulse is Africa’s leading innovative media company, informing and engaging Africa’s young audience - and providing expansive media reach and creative marketing solutions to partners. We run news websites and social channels and marketing and production across Francophone and Anglophone West Africa and East Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Digital Content & Community Manager

Job Location: Maryland, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilties
Content Creation & Scheduling:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Develop and execute a consistent content calendar across all relevant social media platforms, aligned with event timelines and marketing objectives.
	Write compelling, on-brand copy for posts, captions, stories, reels, etc. on all digital touchpoints
	Brief the designer on visual content requirements, providing clear direction on messaging, tone, and creative intent
	Review and approve all content for brand consistency, accuracy, and quality before publishing
	Repurpose event content — highlights, testimonials, behind-the-scenes moments — into evergreen digital assets


Community Management:


	Manage all community channels including social media inboxes, comment sections, WhatsApp broadcast lists, and any owned community platforms
	Respond to direct messages, comments, and enquiries in a timely, warm, and on-brand manner
	Monitor audience sentiment and flag recurring feedback, concerns, or trends to the wider team
	Foster genuine connection and loyalty within the community through consistent, meaningful engagement
	Grow the audience organically through strategic engagement, collaborations, and community-driven campaigns


Live Event Social Media Coverage:


	Lead real-time social media coverage during events, capturing and publishing content that brings the live experience to audiences online
	Coordinate with photographers, videographers, and the wider content team on event day to ensure a steady flow of high-quality assets
	Publish live updates, stories, and reels during events to maximise reach and engagement in real time
	Manage any event-specific hashtags, geotags, and digital activations to drive audience participation


Audience Growth & Engagement Strategy:


	Develop and implement pre- and post-event digital campaigns to build anticipation, drive ticket sales, and sustain audience interest between events
	Identify and engage relevant influencers, brand advocates, and community voices to amplify event reach
	Track and analyse content performance metrics and translate insights into actionable improvements
	Contribute to post-event reports with digital performance data including reach, engagement, follower growth, and sentiment


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Communications, Media Studies, or a related field
	3 – 5 years of experience in social media management, digital content creation, or community management
	Proven experience managing social media for events, lifestyle brands, or entertainment-focused organizations.
	Strong portfolio demonstrating content creation across multiple platforms
	Experience with live event social media coverage is a strong advantage


Skills & Competencies:


	Excellent copywriting skills with a strong sense of tone, voice, and audience
	Deep understanding of social media platforms, algorithms, and content best practices
	Strong visual sensibility and ability to brief and collaborate effectively with designers
	Ability to read analytics and draw meaningful insights
	Highly organised with the ability to manage a content calendar across multiple events simultaneously
	Energetic, personable, and genuinely passionate about community building
	Proficient in social media management tools, Canva or Adobe Suite (basic), and analytics platforms.


Benefits


	Competitive salary based on experience
	Hybrid work mode
	Health Insurance Cover
	Group Life Insurance cover
	Contributory Pension Scheme
	?Data and airtime monthly allowance.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited&nbsp;is a fast-growing renewable energy company based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in providing reliable solar power solutions to homes, businesses, and industries, helping Nigerians achieve energy independence and reduce reliance on generators. We are passionate about clean energy, innovation, and building a sustainable future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Operations Manager to drive efficiency and scale our operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage warehouse, logistics, field operations, and supply chain
	Lead process improvements and cost control initiatives
	Supervise and develop team performance using KPIs
	Ensure compliance with safety, labour laws, and operational standards


Requirements


	5+ years of operations experience (energy, logistics, or manufacturing preferred)
	Degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Supply Chain, or related field
	Strong leadership, problem-solving, and people management skills
	Experience with inventory systems or ERP is a plus.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 monthly.

Note


	Select&nbsp;&ldquo;Operations Manager&rdquo; as your role in the form.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 7 &ndash; 10 working days.

Operations Manager Job at GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited




        
    

        
        GPGATE Solar and Power Nigeria Company Limited is a fast-growing renewable energy company based in Lagos, Nigeria. We specialize in providing reliable solar power solutions to homes, businesses, and industries, helping Nigerians achieve energy independence and reduce reliance on generators. We are passionate about clean energy, innovation, and building a sustainable future.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Manager
Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced Operations Manager to drive efficiency and scale our operations.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage warehouse, logistics, field operations, and supply chain
	Lead process improvements and cost control initiatives
	Supervise and develop team performance using KPIs
	Ensure compliance with safety, labour laws, and operational standards


Requirements


	5+ years of operations experience (energy, logistics, or manufacturing preferred)
	Degree in Business Administration, Engineering, Supply Chain, or related field
	Strong leadership, problem-solving, and people management skills
	Experience with inventory systems or ERP is a plus.


Salary
N300,000 - N400,000 monthly.

Note


	Select “Operations Manager” as your role in the form.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted within 7 – 10 working days.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Social Media Manager",
  "description": "House of Maryjane is a unique brand that offers professional fashion designing services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Festac, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are currently looking for competent and hard working Social media manager to handle all our social media platforms


Job Description


	Deliberate planning, strategy and goal setting
	Scheduling meetings and appointment
	Development of brand awareness and online reputation
	Content management (on all social media platforms)
	SEO (search engine optimization) and generation of inbound traffic
	Cultivation of leads and sales
	Reputation management: Must bea highly motivated, creative individual with experience and a passion for connecting with current and future customers.
	Have good knowledge in using capcut,Ai and other editing tools
	Must be able to work without supervision
	Must be computer literate.


Requirements


	SSCE / OND / HND / BSc. Degree.


Salary
N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;houseofmaryjane1@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note:&nbsp;For more inquiries, call: 08154113937.",

Social Media Manager Job at House of Maryjane




        
    

        
        House of Maryjane is a unique brand that offers professional fashion designing services.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Social Media Manager

Job Location: Festac, Lagos

Job Summary


	We are currently looking for competent and hard working Social media manager to handle all our social media platforms


Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Deliberate planning, strategy and goal setting
	Scheduling meetings and appointment
	Development of brand awareness and online reputation
	Content management (on all social media platforms)
	SEO (search engine optimization) and generation of inbound traffic
	Cultivation of leads and sales
	Reputation management: Must bea highly motivated, creative individual with experience and a passion for connecting with current and future customers.
	Have good knowledge in using capcut,Ai and other editing tools
	Must be able to work without supervision
	Must be computer literate.


Requirements


	SSCE / OND / HND / BSc. Degree.


Salary
N200,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note: For more inquiries, call: 08154113937.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant CLO Coordinator",
  "description": "The U.S. Mission to Nigeria comprises of the Embassy in Abuja, FCT, and the Consulate General in Lagos. U.S. Consulate General Lagos is the Mission&rsquo;s representative to the Nigerian people in the southern region.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Assistant CLO Coordinator

Job Location:&nbsp;Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary


	The U.S. Embassy Abuja, Nigeria is seeking eligible and qualified applicants for the Assistant CLO Coordinator position in the Community Liaison Office.


Duties


	The Assistant Community Liaison Office Coordinator (Assistant CLO, A / CLO) supports the CLO Coordinator in fulfilling all Eight Areas of CLO Responsibility (family member employment; crisis management and security; education; communications and outreach; guidance and referral; welcoming, orientation, and departures; community liaison; and events planning), and in developing and managing post programs to enhance community morale.
	The Assistant CLO works with the CLO Coordinator to identify needs within the diverse post community and responds with effective programming, information and resources, and referrals.
	The incumbent works under the supervision of the CLO Coordinator and is expected to interact independently with CLO clients to develop CLO materials and programs, to formulate advocacy plans, and to generate ideas for community events.
	In the absence of a CLO Coordinator, the Assistant CLO will serve as back-up to fulfill all responsibilities.


Job Knowledge


	A good understanding of the Foreign Service community and the structure of the federal government is required.
	Knowledge of general principles of program management and program monitoring is required.
	Standard knowledge of Microsoft Office is required.
	Knowledge of the Department of State&#39;s programs and policies pertaining to CLO Coordinator&#39;s Eight Areas of Responsibility (community liaison, crisis management, employment, education, guidance and referral, communications and outreach, welcoming / orientation / departures, and events planning) is required.


Requirements


	All selected candidates must be able to obtain and hold a top secret level security clearance.&nbsp;
	All selected candidates will be subject to a background&nbsp;investigation and may be subject to a pre-employment medical exam.&nbsp;&nbsp;
	Selected candidate must be able to begin working within a reasonable period of time (6 weeks) upon receipt of agency authorization and/or clearances/certifications or their candidacy may end.


Education Requirements:


	Completion of at least two (2) years of college or university studies is required.


Evaluations:


	You will be evaluated against the qualifications and requirements in this vacancy announcement.&nbsp;
	You may be asked to complete a pre-employment language or skills test.
	You must complete the application form and provide the required documents.&nbsp; Your application must address all the position qualifications and requirements including education, experience, language, and any knowledge/skills/abilities listed.&nbsp; If you fail to submit a complete application, then your application may be disqualified for this position.


Experience:


	A minimum of three (3) years of professional experience, including but not limited to administration, office management, customer service, program management, or public policy is required.
	Please address this factor in your ERA application under Major Duties and Responsibilities.


Language:&nbsp;


	Good working knowledge; speaking, reading &amp; writing of English is required.
	Please address this language requirement in your ERA application.&nbsp;


Skills And Abilities:


	The&nbsp;Assistant CLO is often a front-line responder in addressing quality of life issue for individuals or the broader post community.
	The Assistant CLO must exercise good judgment when advocating for diverse employees and family members and dealing with confidential matters.&nbsp;
	Must possess excellent interpersonal skills and ability to interact well with others, demonstrating good teamwork.&nbsp;
	Must be able to work in a high-stress, fast-paced work environment.&nbsp;
	Must possess experience in data collection and report writing.


Salary

Assistant CLO Coordinator Job at U.S Embassy




        
    

        
        The U.S. Mission to Nigeria comprises of the Embassy in Abuja, FCT, and the Consulate General in Lagos. U.S. Consulate General Lagos is the Mission’s representative to the Nigerian people in the southern region.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant CLO Coordinator

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full Time

Job Summary


	The U.S. Embassy Abuja, Nigeria is seeking eligible and qualified applicants for the Assistant CLO Coordinator position in the Community Liaison Office.


Duties
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Assistant Community Liaison Office Coordinator (Assistant CLO, A / CLO) supports the CLO Coordinator in fulfilling all Eight Areas of CLO Responsibility (family member employment; crisis management and security; education; communications and outreach; guidance and referral; welcoming, orientation, and departures; community liaison; and events planning), and in developing and managing post programs to enhance community morale.
	The Assistant CLO works with the CLO Coordinator to identify needs within the diverse post community and responds with effective programming, information and resources, and referrals.
	The incumbent works under the supervision of the CLO Coordinator and is expected to interact independently with CLO clients to develop CLO materials and programs, to formulate advocacy plans, and to generate ideas for community events.
	In the absence of a CLO Coordinator, the Assistant CLO will serve as back-up to fulfill all responsibilities.


Job Knowledge


	A good understanding of the Foreign Service community and the structure of the federal government is required.
	Knowledge of general principles of program management and program monitoring is required.
	Standard knowledge of Microsoft Office is required.
	Knowledge of the Department of State's programs and policies pertaining to CLO Coordinator's Eight Areas of Responsibility (community liaison, crisis management, employment, education, guidance and referral, communications and outreach, welcoming / orientation / departures, and events planning) is required.


Requirements


	All selected candidates must be able to obtain and hold a top secret level security clearance. 
	All selected candidates will be subject to a background investigation and may be subject to a pre-employment medical exam.  
	Selected candidate must be able to begin working within a reasonable period of time (6 weeks) upon receipt of agency authorization and/or clearances/certifications or their candidacy may end.


Education Requirements:


	Completion of at least two (2) years of college or university studies is required.


Evaluations:


	You will be evaluated against the qualifications and requirements in this vacancy announcement. 
	You may be asked to complete a pre-employment language or skills test.
	You must complete the application form and provide the required documents.  Your application must address all the position qualifications and requirements including education, experience, language, and any knowledge/skills/abilities listed.  If you fail to submit a complete application, then your application may be disqualified for this position.


Experience:


	A minimum of three (3) years of professional experience, including but not limited to administration, office management, customer service, program management, or public policy is required.
	Please address this factor in your ERA application under Major Duties and Responsibilities.


Language: 


	Good working knowledge; speaking, reading & writing of English is required.
	Please address this language requirement in your ERA application. 


Skills And Abilities:


	The Assistant CLO is often a front-line responder in addressing quality of life issue for individuals or the broader post community.
	The Assistant CLO must exercise good judgment when advocating for diverse employees and family members and dealing with confidential matters. 
	Must possess excellent interpersonal skills and ability to interact well with others, demonstrating good teamwork. 
	Must be able to work in a high-stress, fast-paced work environment. 
	Must possess experience in data collection and report writing.


Salary
USD $48,708 / Biweekly.
